gaps: A local command that explains why an already-materialized open event survived or failed the predict-scope filters would make sparse SCOTUS cases easier to triage without querying hidden case facts.
helpful: The provisioned snapshot path kept the case-fact boundary clear.
fedcourts stats provided a useful SCOTUS base-rate anchor.
The local scope and outcome code documented how sparse historical SCOTUS records should be interpreted.
notes: I used aggregate corpus stats and a broad SCOTUS prior query for calibration, but did not fetch new docket facts about this case.
